title: Columnea tigrina
intro: Columnea tigrina, also known as Tiger Columnea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Columnea tigrina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory infections, coughs, and sore throats, as well as to boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Columnea tigrina is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. The genus Columnea belongs to the order Lamiales. This plant is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Columnea tigrina includes kingdom Plantae, division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Lamiales. The family Gesneriaceae encompasses a diverse group of flowering plants.
